statement.en: A Scale-Adaptive Framework for Joint Spatiotemporal Super-Resolution with Diffusion Models
statement.summary: Deep-learning video super-resolution has progressed rapidly, but climate applications typically super-resolve (increase resolution) either space or time, and joint spatiotemporal models are often designed for a single pair of super-resolution (SR) factors (upscaling spatial and temporal ratio between the low-resolution sequence and the high-resolution sequence), limiting transfer across spatial resolutions and temporal cadences (frame rates). We present a scale-adaptive framework that reuses the same architecture across factors by decomposing spatiotemporal SR into a deterministic prediction of the conditional mean, with attention, and a residual conditional diffusion model, with an optional mass-conservation (same precipitation amount in inputs and outputs) transform to preserve aggregate

proposer: Max Defez, Filippo Quarenghi, Mathieu Vrac et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom describes a novel technical architecture (scale-adaptive diffusion for spatiotemporal SR) presented as a preprint dated 2026-04-25. It is not yet peer-reviewed or validated in the standard literature corpus. The claims are internally coherent and plausible given the state of deep learning (as of the knowledge cutoff), but lack independent replication and community consensus. The work is contingent on empirical validation of generalization claims across domains (climate applications), which remain provisional.
